given: The S3 down migration (a single-step downgrade -1 from S3 head) has been executed
when: We inspect the stock table schema
then: The batch_number and serial_number columns are STILL PRESENT (preserved). S3.down reconstructs inventory_code and reverses ONLY S3.up; the split columns are owned by the earlier S1 split migration, so S3.down must not drop them.
architectural_notes: Infra: a contract on the stock data-store shape after a SINGLE-STEP S3 rollback. S3.down reverses only S3.up: it reconstructs inventory_code and leaves batch_number/serial_number intact (those columns are owned by the S1 split revision 20260624142037; retiring them belongs to downgrading PAST S1, not to S3.down). Dropping them in S3.down would break up->down->up reversibility and corrupt the schema. Proven by a parent-aware schema diff against the paired Lakebase branch. Cross-cutting concern: reversibility (R4) , each migration down reverses its OWN up.

independence.rationale: AC1-4 test the inventory_code column lifecycle + content; AC5 guards that S3.down does NOT over-reach by retiring columns a prior migration owns (reversibility of the single-step rollback).
